behaviors.description: Generated function signature matches the interface required by the natural-language spec (parameter names/types and return type).
behaviors.justification: Signature correctness is produced by the LLM, and real failures include model misinterpreting the spec or drifting format. A mock can validate parsing logic but cannot expose true model-output errors.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real LLM once with a spec that tightly constrains signature details, compile/parse the generated code, and assert exact signature match against expected interface.

behaviors.description: Generated implementation passes the provided tests on first attempt or after iterative retries.
behaviors.justification: This behavior spans multiple agentic steps (generate -> test -> optional refine -> retest). Real failure boundary includes loop orchestration plus LLM quality across attempts; single-step or mocked tests can miss this.
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end with real LLM and real test execution: assert service either succeeds on attempt 1 or uses retries and eventually returns a passing implementation within 3 attempts.

behaviors.description: On test failure, the next refinement prompt contains failure messages from the failed run.
behaviors.justification: Primary risk is wiring: failed test output not propagated into prompt-construction path. This is best caught by exercising runner + prompt builder + LLM client boundary together; pure unit mocks may miss serialization/format handoff issues.
behaviors.test_description: Use a deterministic failing generated implementation and a real local test runner, intercept the second LLM request payload, and assert failed test messages are included verbatim/structured as required.

behaviors.description: Service stops after 3 failed attempts and returns a structured error containing all attempts and their test results.
behaviors.justification: Failure is mainly retry-limit and result-aggregation logic across modules. Real LLM behavior is not required; controlled failing attempts are sufficient to expose off-by-one, missing-attempt, or malformed-error bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Wire orchestrator with deterministic always-fail generation/test outcomes, execute full retry loop, and assert exactly 3 attempts were made and error payload contains per-attempt test results in expected schema.

behaviors.description: Full generate-test-refine loop solves a task that a single LLM call usually fails.
behaviors.justification: This explicitly validates sequential agentic improvement, not just wiring. Correctness depends on real model behavior over multiple turns and the loop’s ability to convert failures into improved output.
behaviors.test_description: Use a known-hard prompt with baseline evidence that one-shot generation usually fails; run full pipeline with real LLM and real tests, and assert iterative loop achieves a passing implementation where single-call baseline does not.
